{{/* Full-width home page — hero + static question sections */}}
{{/* ── Hero ───────────────────────────────────────────────── */}}

The open source control plane
for AI model serving

The open source declarative control plane for AI models. Kubernetes-driven, vendor neutral, fleet-scale inference.

Get started → API reference

We're taking off fast. See planned enhancements on GitHub.

model-deployment.yaml 
apiVersion: modelplane.ai/v1alpha1
kind: ModelDeployment
metadata:
  name: qwen-demo
  namespace: ml-team
spec:
  replicas: 2
  workers:
    topology:
      tensor: 1
    template:
      spec:
        containers:
        - name: engine
          image: vllm/vllm-openai:v0.7.3
          args:
          - "--model=Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct"
Serving  qwen-demo.ml-team.svc  · 2/2 replicas
{{/* ── Static question sections — heading left, body right ── */}}

What is Modelplane?

Modelplane is the open source declarative control plane for AI models.

Built by Upbound and powered by Crossplane, Modelplane is the open orchestration layer for inference. Open-source, vendor neutral and Kubernetes-driven, Modelplane is the unified intelligent gateway to manage and scale open weight AI inference fleets across clusters.

Modelplane extends Crossplane, the open source control plane for cloud infrastructure, meaning cluster provisioning and infrastructure lifecycle is built-in and battle-tested.

Modelplane handles fleet scheduling, multi-cluster routing, and infrastructure composition in declarative Kubernetes manifests. Deploying a model based on your team's needs is a kubectl apply away.

How do I get started?

Follow the getting started guide to deploy Modelplane on a local kind cluster and serve a model on GKE. Read concepts to understand the resource model, and browse the API reference for field-level documentation.
